NAME

protocol.h - Server packet processing and protocol implementation.

Signal all active clients to clear their displays before next video frame.

Detailed Description

Server packet processing and protocol implementation.

Definition in file server/protocol.h.

Function Documentation

void broadcast_clear_console_to_all_clients (void )

Signal all active clients to clear their displays before next video frame. Sets the needs_display_clear flag for all currently connected and active clients. This is used when the grid layout changes (clients join/leave) to ensure all clients clear their displays before receiving frames with the new layout.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses atomic flag (needs_display_clear) instead of packet queue
  • Send thread checks flag and sends CLEAR_CONSOLE before next video frame
  • Guarantees CLEAR_CONSOLE arrives before new grid layout frame

SYNCHRONIZATION:

  • Acquires g_client_manager_rwlock for reading
  • Uses atomic operations for flag setting (no mutex needed)
  • Thread-safe access to client list

USAGE SCENARIO:

  • Called when active video source count changes
  • Ensures all clients clear before new grid layout is sent
  • Prevents visual artifacts from old content

Note

This function iterates all clients but only flags active ones

Non-blocking - just sets atomic flags

Definition at line 1932 of file server/protocol.c.

1932                                                   {
1933   SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "broadcast_clear_console_to_all_clients() called - unexpected usage");
1934   log_warn("CLEAR_CONSOLE is now sent from render threads, not broadcast");
1935 }








void
  disconnect_client_for_bad_data (client_info_t * client, const char * format,
  ...)


Definition at line 154 of file
  server/protocol.c.


154                                                                                     {
155   if (!client) {
156     return;
157   }
158 
159   protocol_cleanup_thread_locals();
160 
161   // Atomically set the disconnect flag and get the old value
162   // This ensures only one thread proceeds to disconnect (the first one)
163   bool already_requested = atomic_exchange_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested, true);
164   if (already_requested) {
165     return;  // Already disconnecting, don't do it again
166   }
167 
168   char reason[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL] = {0};
169   if (format) {
170     va_list args;
171     va_start(args, format);
172     safe_vsnprintf(reason, sizeof(reason), format, args);
173     va_end(args);
174   } else {
175     SAFE_STRNCPY(reason, "Protocol violation", sizeof(reason));
176   }
177 
178   const char *reason_str = reason[0] != '\0' ? reason : "Protocol violation";
179   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
180 
181   socket_t socket_snapshot = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
182   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
183   acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
184 
185   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
186   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
187     socket_snapshot = client->socket;
188     if (client->crypto_initialized) {
189       crypto_ctx = crypto_handshake_get_context(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
190     }
191   }
192   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
193 
194   // Get transport reference for WebSocket clients
195   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
196   transport_snapshot = client->transport;
197   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
198 
199   // NOTE: Disconnecting a client due to the client's own bad behavior isn't an
200   // error for us, it's desired behavior for us, so we simply warn and do not
201   // have a need for asciichat_errno here.
202   log_warn("Disconnecting client %u due to protocol violation: %s", client_id, reason_str);
203 
204   if (socket_snapshot != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
205     // Protect socket writes with send_mutex to prevent race with send_thread.
206     // This receive_thread and send_thread both write to same socket.
207     mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
208 
209     asciichat_error_t log_result =
210         log_network_message(socket_snapshot, (const struct crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx, LOG_ERROR,
211                             REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT, "Protocol violation: %s", reason_str);
212     if (log_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
213       log_warn("Failed to send remote log to client %u: %s", client_id, asciichat_error_string(log_result));
214     }
215 
216     asciichat_error_t send_result = packet_send_error(socket_snapshot, crypto_ctx, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, reason_str);
217     if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
218       log_warn("Failed to send error packet to client %u: %s", client_id, asciichat_error_string(send_result));
219     }
220 
221     mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
222   } else if (transport_snapshot) {
223     // For WebSocket clients, try to send error via transport
224     log_debug("Sending error to WebSocket client %u via transport", client_id);
225     acip_send_error(transport_snapshot, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, reason_str);
226   }
227 
228   platform_sleep_ms(500);
229 
230   log_debug("Setting active=false in disconnect_client_for_bad_data (client_id=%u, reason=%s)", client_id, reason_str);
231   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
232   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
233   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
234   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
235   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
236 
237   if (client->audio_queue) {
238     packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
239   }
240 
241   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
242   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
243     socket_shutdown(client->socket, 2);
244     socket_close(client->socket);
245     client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
246   }
247   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
248 }

void
  handle_audio_batch_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process AUDIO_BATCH packet - store efficiently batched audio
    samples. Handles the optimized audio packet format that bundles multiple
    sample chunks into a single packet. This reduces packet overhead and
    improves network efficiency for audio streaming.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
audio_batch_packet_t header:





  
 •

  
uint32_t batch_count: Number of sample chunks in this batch

  
 •

  
uint32_t total_samples: Total number of float samples

  
 •

  
uint32_t sample_rate: Samples per second (typically 44100)

  
 •

  
uint32_t channels: Number of audio channels (1 = mono)





  
•

  
float samples[total_samples]: IEEE 754 sample data




PERFORMANCE ADVANTAGES:


    
  
  • Reduces packet count by 5-10x compared to single audio packets
    • 
  
  • Lower network overhead and CPU context switching
    • 
  
  • Better burst tolerance with larger buffers
    • 



VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Header size matches audio_batch_packet_t
    • 
  
  • Total packet size matches header + samples
    • 
  
  • Sample count is within reasonable bounds
    • 
  
  • Client is authorized to send audio
    • 



BUFFER MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Extracts samples from packet payload
    • 
  
  • Stores in client->incoming_audio_buffer (same as single format)
    • 
  
  • Ring buffer automatically handles overflow
    • 
  
  • mixer.c consumes batched samples identically
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid batch headers are logged and packet dropped
    • 
  
  • Oversized batches are rejected (prevents DoS)
    • 
  
  • Buffer allocation failures are handled gracefully
    • 



Parameters


client Source client providing batched audio data


data Packet payload containing batch header and samples


len Total size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This is the preferred audio packet format



Batching is transparent to audio processing pipeline




See also


handle_audio_packet() For legacy single-batch
  format



AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES For maximum batch size constant




Definition at line 1188 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1188                                                                                     {
1189   // Log every audio batch packet reception
1190   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Received audio batch packet from client %u (len=%zu, is_sending_audio=%d)",
1191                   client->client_id, len, atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_audio));
1192 
1193   VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "AUDIO_BATCH");
1194   VALIDATE_MIN_SIZE(client, len, sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t), "AUDIO_BATCH");
1195   VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "AUDIO_BATCH");
1196 
1197   // Parse batch header using utility function
1198   audio_batch_info_t batch_info;
1199   asciichat_error_t parse_result = audio_parse_batch_header(data, len, &batch_info);
1200   if (parse_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1201     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Failed to parse audio batch header");
1202     return;
1203   }
1204 
1205   uint32_t packet_batch_count = batch_info.batch_count;
1206   uint32_t total_samples = batch_info.total_samples;
1207   uint32_t sample_rate = batch_info.sample_rate;
1208 
1209   (void)packet_batch_count;
1210   (void)sample_rate;
1211 
1212   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, packet_batch_count, "batch_count", "AUDIO_BATCH");
1213   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, total_samples, "total_samples", "AUDIO_BATCH");
1214 
1215   size_t samples_bytes = 0;
1216   if (safe_size_mul(total_samples, sizeof(uint32_t), &samples_bytes)) {
1217     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_BATCH sample size overflow (samples=%u)", total_samples);
1218     return;
1219   }
1220 
1221   size_t expected_size = sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t) + samples_bytes;
1222   if (len != expected_size) {
1223     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_BATCH length mismatch: got %zu expected %zu", len, expected_size);
1224     return;
1225   }
1226 
1227   // Bounds check to prevent integer overflow on allocation
1228   // Maximum allowed samples: AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES * 2 (2048 samples)
1229   // This prevents total_samples * sizeof(float) from exceeding 8KB
1230   const uint32_t MAX_AUDIO_SAMPLES = AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES * 2;
1231   if (total_samples > MAX_AUDIO_SAMPLES) {
1232     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_BATCH too many samples: %u (max: %u)", total_samples,
1233                                    MAX_AUDIO_SAMPLES);
1234     return;
1235   }
1236 
1237   const uint8_t *samples_ptr = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t);
1238 
1239   // Safe allocation: total_samples is bounded above, so multiplication won't overflow
1240   size_t alloc_size = (size_t)total_samples * sizeof(float);
1241   float *samples = SAFE_MALLOC(alloc_size, float *);
1242   if (!samples) {
1243     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for audio sample conversion");
1244     return;
1245   }
1246 
1247   // Use helper function to dequantize samples
1248   asciichat_error_t dq_result = audio_dequantize_samples(samples_ptr, total_samples, samples);
1249   if (dq_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1250     SAFE_FREE(samples);
1251     return;
1252   }
1253 
1254 #ifndef NDEBUG
1255   static int recv_count = 0;
1256   recv_count++;
1257   if (recv_count % 100 == 0) {
1258     uint32_t raw0 = bytes_read_u32_unaligned(samples_ptr + 0 * sizeof(uint32_t));
1259     uint32_t raw1 = bytes_read_u32_unaligned(samples_ptr + 1 * sizeof(uint32_t));
1260     uint32_t raw2 = bytes_read_u32_unaligned(samples_ptr + 2 * sizeof(uint32_t));
1261     int32_t scaled0 = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(raw0);
1262     int32_t scaled1 = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(raw1);
1263     int32_t scaled2 = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(raw2);
1264     log_info("RECV: network[0]=0x%08x, network[1]=0x%08x, network[2]=0x%08x", raw0, raw1, raw2);
1265     log_info("RECV: scaled[0]=%d, scaled[1]=%d, scaled[2]=%d", scaled0, scaled1, scaled2);
1266     log_info("RECV: samples[0]=%.6f, samples[1]=%.6f, samples[2]=%.6f", samples[0], samples[1], samples[2]);
1267   }
1268 #endif
1269 
1270   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1271     asciichat_error_t write_result = audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, samples, total_samples);
1272     if (write_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1273       log_error("Failed to write decoded audio batch to buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(write_result));
1274     }
1275   }
1276 
1277   SAFE_FREE(samples);
1278 }

    audio_dequantize_samples(), audio_parse_batch_header(),
    audio_ring_buffer_write(), audio_batch_info_t::batch_count,
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    audio_batch_info_t::sample_rate, and
    audio_batch_info_t::total_samples.






void
  handle_audio_opus_batch_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH packet - efficient Opus-encoded audio
    batch from client. Handles batched Opus-encoded audio frames sent by the
    client. This provides ~98% bandwidth reduction compared to raw PCM audio
    while maintaining excellent audio quality.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
opus_batch_header_t (16 bytes):





  
 •

  
sample_rate (4 bytes)

  
 •

  
frame_duration (4 bytes)

  
 •

  
frame_count (4 bytes)

  
 •

  
reserved (4 bytes)





  
•

  
Opus encoded data (variable size, typically ~60 bytes/frame @ 24kbps)




PROCESSING FLOW:



  
1.

  
Parse batch header

  
2.

  
Decode each Opus frame back to PCM samples (960 samples/frame @
    48kHz)

  
3.

  
Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer




PERFORMANCE:


    
  
  • Bandwidth: ~60 bytes/frame vs ~3840 bytes/frame for raw PCM (64:1
      compression)
    • 
  
  • Quality: Excellent at 24 kbps VOIP mode
    • 
  
  • Latency: 20ms frames for real-time audio
    • 



Parameters


client Client that sent the audio packet


data Packet payload data


len Packet payload length


See also


av_receive_audio_opus_batch() For packet parsing



opus_codec_decode() For Opus decoding




handle_audio_batch_packet() For raw PCM audio batch
    handling




Definition at line 1315 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1315                                                                                          {
1316   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "Received Opus audio batch from client %u (len=%zu)", client->client_id, len);
1317 
1318   VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1319   VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1320   VALIDATE_RESOURCE_INITIALIZED(client, client->opus_decoder, "Opus decoder");
1321 
1322   // Parse Opus batch packet
1323   const uint8_t *opus_data = NULL;
1324   size_t opus_size = 0;
1325   const uint16_t *frame_sizes = NULL;
1326   int sample_rate = 0;
1327   int frame_duration = 0;
1328   int frame_count = 0;
1329 
1330   asciichat_error_t result = packet_parse_opus_batch(data, len, &opus_data, &opus_size, &frame_sizes, &sample_rate,
1331                                                      &frame_duration, &frame_count);
1332 
1333   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1334     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Failed to parse AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH packet");
1335     return;
1336   }
1337 
1338   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, frame_count, "frame_count", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1339   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, opus_size, "opus_size", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1340 
1341   // Calculate samples per frame (20ms @ 48kHz = 960 samples)
1342   int samples_per_frame = (sample_rate * frame_duration) / 1000;
1343   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, samples_per_frame, 1, 4096, "samples_per_frame", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1344 
1345   // Use static buffer for common case to avoid malloc in hot path
1346   // Typical batches: 1-32 frames of 960 samples = up to 30,720 samples
1347   // Static buffer holds 32 frames @ 48kHz 20ms = 30,720 samples (120KB)
1348 #define OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES (32 * 960)
1349   static float static_decode_buffer[OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES];
1350 
1351   size_t total_samples = (size_t)samples_per_frame * (size_t)frame_count;
1352   float *decoded_samples;
1353   bool used_malloc = false;
1354 
1355   if (total_samples <= OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES) {
1356     decoded_samples = static_decode_buffer;
1357   } else {
1358     // Unusual large batch - fall back to malloc
1359     log_warn("Client %u: Large audio batch requires malloc (%zu samples)", client->client_id, total_samples);
1360     decoded_samples = SAFE_MALLOC(total_samples * sizeof(float), float *);
1361     if (!decoded_samples) {
1362       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for Opus decoded samples");
1363       return;
1364     }
1365     used_malloc = true;
1366   }
1367 
1368   // Decode each Opus frame using frame_sizes array
1369   int total_decoded = 0;
1370   size_t opus_offset = 0;
1371 
1372   for (int i = 0; i < frame_count; i++) {
1373     // Get exact frame size from frame_sizes array (convert from network byte order)
1374     size_t frame_size = (size_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(frame_sizes[i]);
1375 
1376     // DEBUG: Log the actual bytes of each Opus frame
1377     if (frame_size > 0) {
1378       log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Client %u: Opus frame %d: size=%zu, first_bytes=[0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x]",
1379                       client->client_id, i, frame_size, opus_data[opus_offset] & 0xFF,
1380                       frame_size > 1 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 1] & 0xFF) : 0,
1381                       frame_size > 2 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 2] & 0xFF) : 0,
1382                       frame_size > 3 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 3] & 0xFF) : 0);
1383     }
1384 
1385     if (opus_offset + frame_size > opus_size) {
1386       log_error("Client %u: Frame %d size overflow (offset=%zu, frame_size=%zu, total=%zu)", client->client_id, i + 1,
1387                 opus_offset, frame_size, opus_size);
1388       if (used_malloc) {
1389         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1390       }
1391       return;
1392     }
1393 
1394     // SECURITY: Bounds check before writing decoded samples to prevent buffer overflow
1395     // An attacker could send malicious Opus frames that decode to more samples than expected
1396     if ((size_t)total_decoded + (size_t)samples_per_frame > total_samples) {
1397       log_error("Client %u: Opus decode would overflow buffer (decoded=%d, frame_samples=%d, max=%zu)",
1398                 client->client_id, total_decoded, samples_per_frame, total_samples);
1399       if (used_malloc) {
1400         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1401       }
1402       return;
1403     }
1404 
1405     int decoded_count = opus_codec_decode((opus_codec_t *)client->opus_decoder, &opus_data[opus_offset], frame_size,
1406                                           &decoded_samples[total_decoded], samples_per_frame);
1407 
1408     if (decoded_count < 0) {
1409       log_error("Client %u: Opus decoding failed for frame %d/%d (size=%zu)", client->client_id, i + 1, frame_count,
1410                 frame_size);
1411       if (used_malloc) {
1412         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1413       }
1414       return;
1415     }
1416 
1417     total_decoded += decoded_count;
1418     opus_offset += frame_size;
1419   }
1420 
1421   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Client %u: Decoded %d Opus frames -> %d samples", client->client_id, frame_count,
1422                   total_decoded);
1423 
1424   // DEBUG: Log sample values to detect all-zero issue
1425   static int server_decode_count = 0;
1426   server_decode_count++;
1427   if (total_decoded > 0 && (server_decode_count <= 10 || server_decode_count % 100 == 0)) {
1428     float peak = 0.0f, rms = 0.0f;
1429     for (int i = 0; i < total_decoded && i < 100; i++) {
1430       float abs_val = fabsf(decoded_samples[i]);
1431       if (abs_val > peak)
1432         peak = abs_val;
1433       rms += decoded_samples[i] * decoded_samples[i];
1434     }
1435     rms = sqrtf(rms / (total_decoded > 100 ? 100 : total_decoded));
1436     // Log first 4 bytes of Opus data to compare with client encode
1437     log_info("SERVER OPUS DECODE #%d from client %u: decoded_rms=%.6f, opus_first4=[0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x]",
1438              server_decode_count, client->client_id, rms, opus_size > 0 ? opus_data[0] : 0,
1439              opus_size > 1 ? opus_data[1] : 0, opus_size > 2 ? opus_data[2] : 0, opus_size > 3 ? opus_data[3] : 0);
1440   }
1441 
1442   // Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer
1443   // Note: audio_ring_buffer_write returns error code, not sample count
1444   // Buffer overflow warnings are logged inside audio_ring_buffer_write if buffer is full
1445   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer && total_decoded > 0) {
1446     asciichat_error_t result = audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, decoded_samples, total_decoded);
1447     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1448       log_error("Client %u: Failed to write decoded audio to buffer: %d", client->client_id, result);
1449     }
1450   }
1451 
1452   if (used_malloc) {
1453     SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1454   }
1455 }






References audio_ring_buffer_write(),
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(), opus_codec_decode(),

  packet_parse_opus_batch().






void
  handle_audio_opus_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process AUDIO_OPUS packet - decode single Opus frame from client.
    Handles single Opus-encoded audio frames sent by clients. The packet format
    includes a 16-byte header followed by the Opus-encoded data.




PACKET STRUCTURE:


    
  
  • Offset 0: sample_rate (uint32_t, native byte order - bug in client)
    • 
  
  • Offset 4: frame_duration (uint32_t, native byte order - bug in
    client)
    • 
  
  • Offset 8: reserved (8 bytes)
    • 
  
  • Offset 16: Opus-encoded audio data
    • 



Parameters


client Client info structure


data Packet payload


len Packet length


Definition at line 1475 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1475                                                                                    {
1476   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Received Opus audio from client %u (len=%zu)", client->client_id, len);
1477 
1478   if (VALIDATE_PACKET_NOT_NULL(client, data, "AUDIO_OPUS")) {
1479     return;
1480   }
1481 
1482   // Minimum size: 16-byte header + at least 1 byte of Opus data
1483   if (len < 17) {
1484     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS packet too small: %zu bytes", len);
1485     return;
1486   }
1487 
1488   if (!atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_audio)) {
1489     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS received before audio stream enabled");
1490     return;
1491   }
1492 
1493   if (!client->opus_decoder) {
1494     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Opus decoder not initialized");
1495     return;
1496   }
1497 
1498   // Parse header (16 bytes) - convert from network byte order
1499   const uint8_t *buf = (const uint8_t *)data;
1500   uint32_t sample_rate_net, frame_duration_net;
1501   memcpy(&sample_rate_net, buf, 4);
1502   memcpy(&frame_duration_net, buf + 4, 4);
1503   uint32_t sample_rate = NET_TO_HOST_U32(sample_rate_net);
1504   uint32_t frame_duration = NET_TO_HOST_U32(frame_duration_net);
1505 
1506   // Extract Opus data (after 16-byte header)
1507   const uint8_t *opus_data = buf + 16;
1508   size_t opus_size = len - 16;
1509 
1510   // Validate parameters
1511   if (sample_rate == 0 || sample_rate > 192000) {
1512     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid sample_rate: %u", sample_rate);
1513     return;
1514   }
1515 
1516   if (frame_duration == 0 || frame_duration > 120) {
1517     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid frame_duration: %u ms", frame_duration);
1518     return;
1519   }
1520 
1521   // Calculate expected samples per frame
1522   int samples_per_frame = (int)((sample_rate * frame_duration) / 1000);
1523   if (samples_per_frame <= 0 || samples_per_frame > 5760) { // Max 120ms @ 48kHz
1524     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid samples_per_frame: %d", samples_per_frame);
1525     return;
1526   }
1527 
1528   // Decode Opus frame
1529   float decoded_samples[5760]; // Max Opus frame size (120ms @ 48kHz)
1530   int decoded_count =
1531       opus_codec_decode((opus_codec_t *)client->opus_decoder, opus_data, opus_size, decoded_samples, samples_per_frame);
1532 
1533   if (decoded_count < 0) {
1534     log_error("Client %u: Opus decoding failed (size=%zu)", client->client_id, opus_size);
1535     return;
1536   }
1537 
1538   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_VERY_FAST, "Client %u: Decoded Opus frame -> %d samples", client->client_id, decoded_count);
1539 
1540   // Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer
1541   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer && decoded_count > 0) {
1542     asciichat_error_t write_result =
1543         audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, decoded_samples, decoded_count);
1544     if (write_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1545       log_error("Failed to write decoded Opus samples to buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(write_result));
1546     }
1547   }
1548 }






References atomic_load_bool(),
    audio_ring_buffer_write(), disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    and opus_codec_decode().






void
  handle_audio_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process AUDIO packet - store single audio sample batch (legacy
    format). Handles the original audio packet format that sends one batch of
    float samples per packet. This format is less efficient than AUDIO_BATCH but
    still supported for backward compatibility.




PACKET STRUCTURE:


    
  
  • float samples[len/sizeof(float)] (IEEE 754 format)
    • 
  
  • Sample rate assumed to be 44100 Hz
    • 
  
  • Mono audio (single channel)
    • 



PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Less efficient than handle_audio_batch_packet()
    • 
  
  • Higher packet overhead per sample
    • 
  
  • Still real-time capable for typical loads
    • 



BUFFER MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Stores samples in client->incoming_audio_buffer (lock-free ring
    buffer)
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow drops oldest samples to maintain real-time behavior
    • 
  
  • mixer.c consumes samples for multi-client audio mixing
    • 



STATE VALIDATION:


    
  
  • Only processes if client->is_sending_audio is true
    • 
  
  • Requires valid buffer pointer and non-zero length
    • 
  
  • Handles buffer pointer safely during shutdown
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packets are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow is handled by ring buffer (drops old data)
    • 
  
  • Graceful shutdown behavior
    • 



Parameters


client Source client providing audio data


data Packet payload containing float audio samples


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


Prefer AUDIO_BATCH format for better efficiency



Audio processing happens in mixer threads, not here




See also


handle_audio_batch_packet() For efficient batched
  format



audio_ring_buffer_write() For storage implementation




Definition at line 1091 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1091                                                                               {
1092   VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "AUDIO");
1093   VALIDATE_AUDIO_ALIGNMENT(client, len, sizeof(float), "AUDIO");
1094   VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "AUDIO");
1095 
1096   int num_samples = (int)(len / sizeof(float));
1097   VALIDATE_AUDIO_SAMPLE_COUNT(client, num_samples, AUDIO_SAMPLES_PER_PACKET, "AUDIO");
1098   VALIDATE_RESOURCE_INITIALIZED(client, client->incoming_audio_buffer, "audio buffer");
1099 
1100   const float *samples = (const float *)data;
1101   asciichat_error_t result = audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, samples, num_samples);
1102   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1103     log_error("Failed to write audio samples to buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1104   }
1105 }

void
  handle_client_capabilities_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet - configure client-specific
    rendering. This packet contains detailed information about the client's
    terminal capabilities and preferences. The server uses this data to generate
    appropriately formatted ASCII art and ANSI escape sequences.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
terminal_capabilities_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
width, height: Terminal dimensions in characters

  
 •

  
capabilities: Bitmask of terminal features

  
 •

  
color_level: ANSI color support level (1, 8, 16, 256, 24-bit)

  
 •

  
color_count: Number of supported colors

  
 •

  
render_mode: Foreground, background, or half-block rendering

  
 •

  
term_type: $TERM environment variable value

  
 •

  
colorterm: $COLORTERM environment variable value

  
 •

  
utf8_support: Whether terminal supports UTF-8

  
 •

  
palette_type: ASCII character palette preference

  
 •

  
palette_custom: Custom character set if PALETTE_CUSTOM




STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Updates client dimensions (width, height)
    • 
  
  • Stores complete terminal capabilities structure
    • 
  
  • Initializes per-client ASCII palette cache
    • 
  
  • Sets client->has_terminal_caps = true
    • 



PALETTE INITIALIZATION: The function performs critical palette
    setup:



  
1.

  
Determines character set based on palette_type

  
2.

  
Handles custom palettes if provided

  
3.

  
Generates luminance-to-character mapping

  
4.

  
Caches results for fast ASCII generation




THREAD SAFETY:


    
  
  • All client state updates are mutex-protected
    • 
  
  • Uses client->client_state_mutex for atomicity
    • 
  
  • Safe to call concurrently with render threads
    • 



VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Packet size matches expected structure
    • 
  
  • String fields are safely copied with bounds checking
    • 
  
  • Palette initialization is verified
    • 
  
  • Network byte order conversion
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packets are logged and ignored
    • 
  
  • Palette initialization failures use server defaults
    • 
  
  • Missing capabilities default to safe values
    • 



INTEGRATION IMPACT:


    
  
  • render.c uses capabilities for ASCII generation
    • 
  
  • Color output depends on client's color_level
    • 
  
  • Character selection uses initialized palette
    • 



Parameters


client Target client whose capabilities are being
  configured


data Packet payload containing terminal_capabilities_packet_t


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This packet is typically sent once after CLIENT_JOIN



Capabilities can be updated during the session




Changes affect all subsequent ASCII frame generation




See also


initialize_client_palette() For palette setup
  details



terminal_color_level_name() For color level descriptions

  server/protocol.c.

1671   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, caps->desired_fps, 1, 144, "desired_fps", "CLIENT_CAPABILITIES");

1698 
1699   // Extract wants_padding flag (1=padding enabled, 0=no padding for snapshot/piped modes)
1700   client->terminal_caps.wants_padding = (caps->wants_padding != 0);
1701 
1702   const char *custom_chars =
1703       (client->terminal_caps.palette_type == PALETTE_CUSTOM && client->terminal_caps.palette_custom[0])

1708                                 client->client_palette_chars, &client->client_palette_len,
1709                                 client->client_luminance_palette) == 0) {

1717   }
1718

1723            client->codec_capabilities_video, client->codec_capabilities_audio);
1724 
1725   client->has_terminal_caps = true;
1726

1728            "render_mode=%s, reliable=%s, fps=%u, wants_padding=%d",
1729            client->client_id, client->width, client->height,
1730            terminal_color_level_name(client->terminal_caps.color_level), client->terminal_caps.color_count,
1731            client->terminal_caps.capabilities, client->terminal_caps.term_type, client->terminal_caps.colorterm,
1732            (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK
1733                 ? "half-block"
1734                 : (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND ? "background" : "foreground")),
1735            client->terminal_caps.detection_reliable ? "yes" : "no", client->terminal_caps.desired_fps,
1736            client->terminal_caps.wants_padding);
1737 
1738   // Send capabilities acknowledgment to client
1739   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1740     log_info_client(client, "Terminal configured: %ux%u, %s, %s mode, %u fps", client->width, client->height,
1741                     terminal_color_level_name(client->terminal_caps.color_level),
1742                     (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK
1743                          ? "half-block"
1744                          : (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND ? "background" : "foreground")),
1745                     client->terminal_caps.desired_fps);
1746   }
1747 
1748   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1749 
1750   log_warn("[CAPS_HANDLER] ✅ CAPS_COMPLETE: client_id=%u - all validations passed, capabilities stored",
1751            client->client_id);

void
  handle_client_join_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process CLIENT_JOIN packet - client announces identity and
    capabilities. This is the first substantive packet clients send after
    establishing a TCP connection. It provides the server with essential
    information for managing the client throughout its session.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
client_info_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
display_name: Human-readable client identifier

  
 •

  
capabilities: Bitmask of CLIENT_CAP_* flags




STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Updates client->display_name from packet
    • 
  
  • Sets client->can_send_video based on CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO
    • 
  
  • Sets client->can_send_audio based on CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO
    • 
  
  • Sets client->wants_stretch based on CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH
    • 



PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Does NOT automatically start media streams (requires STREAM_START)
    • 
  
  • Does NOT send CLEAR_CONSOLE to other clients (prevents flicker)
    • 
  
  • Logs client capabilities for debugging
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Silently ignores packets with wrong size
    • 
  
  • Invalid display names are truncated safely
    • 
  
  • Missing capabilities default to false
    • 



Parameters


client Target client whose state will be updated


data Packet payload (should be client_info_packet_t)


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This function should only be called by client receive
  threads



Client state is already protected by receive thread
  serialization




See also


handle_stream_start_packet() For enabling media
  transmission


Definition at line 292 of file
  server/protocol.c.


292                                                                                     {
293   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(client_info_packet_t), "CLIENT_JOIN");
294 
295   const client_info_packet_t *join_info = (const client_info_packet_t *)data;
296 
297   // Validate display name is present and not just whitespace
298   if (join_info->display_name[0] == '\0') {
299     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "CLIENT_JOIN display_name cannot be empty");
300     return;
301   }
302 
303   uint32_t capabilities = NET_TO_HOST_U32(join_info->capabilities);
304 
305   // Validate at least one capability flag is set
306   const uint32_t VALID_CAP_MASK = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO | CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO | CLIENT_CAP_COLOR | CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
307   VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS(client, capabilities, VALID_CAP_MASK, "CLIENT_JOIN");
308 
309   // Validate no unknown capability bits are set
310   VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK(client, capabilities, VALID_CAP_MASK, "CLIENT_JOIN");
311 
312   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, join_info->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
313 
314   client->can_send_video = (capabilities & CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO) != 0;
315   client->can_send_audio = (capabilities & CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO) != 0;
316   client->wants_stretch = (capabilities & CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH) != 0;
317 
318   log_info("Client %u joined: %s (video=%d, audio=%d, stretch=%d)", client->client_id, client->display_name,
319            client->can_send_video, client->can_send_audio, client->wants_stretch);
320 
321   // Notify client of successful join (encrypted channel)
322   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
323     log_info_client(client, "Joined as '%s' (video=%s, audio=%s)", client->display_name,
324                     client->can_send_video ? "yes" : "no", client->can_send_audio ? "yes" : "no");
325   }
326 }






References disconnect_client_for_bad_data().






void
  handle_client_leave_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process CLIENT_LEAVE packet - handle clean client disconnect.
    Clients may send this packet before disconnecting to allow the server to log
    the disconnect reason and perform clean state management. This is optional
    but preferred over abrupt disconnects.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
Optional string containing disconnect reason (0-256 bytes)




PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Client logs the disconnect reason if provided
    • 
  
  • Server continues normal disconnect sequence after receiving packet
    • 
  
  • Client remains responsible for closing socket
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Empty payload is handled gracefully
    • 
  
  • Oversized payloads are rejected
    • 
  
  • Invalid UTF-8 in reason is handled gracefully (logged as-is)
    • 



Parameters


client Client that sent the leave packet


data Packet payload (optional reason string)


len Size of packet payload in bytes (0-256)


Note


This handler doesn't trigger immediate disconnect



Actual disconnect occurs when socket closes




Definition at line 435 of file
  server/protocol.c.


435                                                                                      {
436   if (!client) {
437     return;
438   }
439 
440   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
441 
442   if (len == 0) {
443     // Empty reason - client disconnecting without explanation
444     log_info("Client %u sent leave notification (no reason)", client_id);
445   } else if (len <= 256) {
446     // Reason provided - extract and log it
447     if (!data) {
448       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Client %u sent leave notification with non-zero length but NULL data", client_id);
449       return;
450     }
451 
452     char reason[257] = {0};
453     memcpy(reason, data, len);
454     reason[len] = '\0';
455 
456     // Validate reason is printable (handle potential non-UTF8 gracefully)
457     bool all_printable = true;
458     for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
459       uint8_t c = (uint8_t)reason[i];
460       if (c < 32 && c != '\t' && c != '\n') {
461         all_printable = false;
462         break;
463       }
464     }
465 
466     if (all_printable) {
467       log_info("Client %u sent leave notification: %s", client_id, reason);
468     } else {
469       log_info("Client %u sent leave notification (reason contains non-printable characters)", client_id);
470     }
471   } else {
472     // Oversized reason - shouldn't happen with validation.h checks
473     log_warn("Client %u sent oversized leave reason (%zu bytes, max 256)", client_id, len);
474   }
475 
476   // Deactivate client to stop processing packets
477   // Sets client->active = false immediately - triggers client cleanup procedures
478   log_debug("Setting active=false in handle_client_leave_packet (client_id=%u)", client_id);
479   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
480 
481   // Note: We don't disconnect the client here - that happens when socket closes
482   // This is just a clean notification before disconnect
483 }






References atomic_store_bool().






void
  handle_image_frame_h265_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process H.265-encoded image frame from client. Handles incoming
    H.265/HEVC-encoded video frames from clients. Frames are decoded to ASCII
    art and added to the compositing pipeline.




FLOW:



  
1.

  
Validate packet structure and minimum size

  
2.

  
Auto-enable video stream on first frame

  
3.

  
Decode H.265 packet to ASCII grid

  
4.

  
Convert ASCII to frame buffer format for rendering

  
5.

  
Store in client's incoming_video_buffer




Parameters


client Client connection info


data H.265 packet payload (includes header with frame dimensions)


len Payload size in bytes


PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height:
  u16][x265_data...]


    
  
  • flags: H265_FLAG_KEYFRAME, H265_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE
    • 
  
  • width/height: Frame dimensions in characters
    • 
  
  • x265_data: H.265-encoded frame data
    • 



Definition at line 937 of file
  server/protocol.c.


937                                                                                          {
938   // Validate codec capability for H.265 frames
939   if (!VIDEO_CODEC_SUPPORTED(client->codec_capabilities_video, VIDEO_CODEC_H265)) {
940     log_error("Client %s sent H.265 frame but does not support VIDEO_CODEC_H265 (capabilities=0x%x)",
941               client->client_id, client->codec_capabilities_video);
942     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "VIDEO_CODEC_H265 not supported by client (capabilities=0x%x)",
943                                    client->codec_capabilities_video);
944     return;
945   }
946 
947   if (!data || len < 5) {
948     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "H265_FRAME payload too small: %zu bytes (min 5)", len);
949     return;
950   }
951 
952   // Auto-enable video stream if not already enabled
953   // Use atomic_exchange to atomically set flag and check old value
954   bool was_sending_video = atomic_exchange_bool(&client->is_sending_video, true);
955   if (!was_sending_video) {
956     log_info("Client %s auto-enabled H.265 video stream (received IMAGE_FRAME_H265)", client->client_id);
957     if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
958       log_info_client(client, "First H.265 video frame received - streaming active");
959     }
960   }
961 
962   uint8_t flags = *(const uint8_t *)data;
963   bool is_keyframe = (flags & 0x01) != 0;
964   bool size_changed = (flags & 0x02) != 0;
965 
966   log_debug("H265_FRAME: flags=0x%02x, keyframe=%s, size_change=%s, len=%zu", flags, is_keyframe ? "yes" : "no",
967             size_changed ? "yes" : "no", len);
968 
969   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
970     log_error("Client %s has no incoming video buffer for H.265 frames", client->client_id);
971     return;
972   }
973 
974   // Get H.265 server context (may be NULL during shutdown)
975   h265_server_context_t *h265_ctx = g_h265_server;
976   if (!h265_ctx) {
977     if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
978       log_error("H.265 codec server context not initialized");
979     }
980     return;
981   }
982 
983   // Get or create decoder for this client
984   uint32_t client_id_num = 0;
985   if (sscanf(client->client_id, "%u", &client_id_num) != 1) {
986     // Fallback to string hash if can't parse as number
987     client_id_num = 0;
988     for (int i = 0; client->client_id[i]; i++) {
989       client_id_num = (client_id_num * 31 + (unsigned char)client->client_id[i]) & 0xFFFFFFFF;
990     }
991   }
992 
993   h265_server_client_t *client_decoder = h265_server_get_client_decoder(h265_ctx, client_id_num);
994   if (!client_decoder) {
995     log_error("Failed to get H.265 decoder for client %s", client->client_id);
996     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Failed to initialize H.265 decoder");
997     return;
998   }
999 
1000   // Allocate temporary RGBA buffer for decoding
1001   // H.265 frames from client are sent as [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_data...]
1002   // Max frame is (256x256 = 65536 pixels) * 4 bytes/pixel = 262KB
1003   uint8_t *rgba_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(512 * 512 * 4, uint8_t *);
1004   if (!rgba_buf) {
1005     log_error("Failed to allocate RGBA buffer for H.265 decoding");
1006     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Out of memory for H.265 frame decoding");
1007     return;
1008   }
1009 
1010   uint16_t frame_width = 0;
1011   uint16_t frame_height = 0;
1012   size_t rgba_size = 512 * 512 * 4;
1013 
1014   // Decode H.265 packet to RGBA
1015   asciichat_error_t decode_result = h265_server_decode_and_convert(client_decoder, (const uint8_t *)data, len, rgba_buf,
1016                                                                    &frame_width, &frame_height, &rgba_size);
1017 
1018   if (decode_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1019     log_error("H.265 decoding failed for client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(decode_result));
1020     SAFE_FREE(rgba_buf);
1021     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "H.265 frame decode error");
1022     return;
1023   }
1024 
1025   // Write decoded frame to video buffer
1026   video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_begin_write(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1027   if (frame && frame->data) {
1028     memcpy(frame->data, rgba_buf, rgba_size);
1029     frame->size = rgba_size;
1030     frame->width = frame_width;
1031     frame->height = frame_height;
1032     frame->is_keyframe = is_keyframe;
1033 
1034     // Update frame metadata
1035     frame->receive_timestamp_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1036     frame->sequence_number = client->frames_received++;
1037 
1038     log_info_every(100, "RECV_H265 #%lu: client_id=%s size=%zu dims=%ux%u keyframe=%s", frame->sequence_number,
1039                    client->client_id, rgba_size, frame_width, frame_height, is_keyframe ? "yes" : "no");
1040 
1041     video_frame_commit(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1042   } else {
1043     log_warn("Failed to get write buffer for client %s (frame=%p, frame->data=%p)", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
1044              frame ? frame->data : NULL);
1045   }
1046 
1047   SAFE_FREE(rgba_buf);
1048 }






References atomic_exchange_bool(),
    atomic_load_bool(), disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    frame, g_h265_server, g_should_exit,
    h265_server_decode_and_convert(),
    h265_server_get_client_decoder(), time_get_realtime_ns(),
    video_frame_begin_write(), and video_frame_commit().






void
  handle_image_frame_packet (client_info_t * client, void * data, size_t
  len)


Process IMAGE_FRAME packet - store client's video data for
    rendering. This is the most performance-critical packet handler, processing
    real-time video data from clients. It validates, stores, and tracks video
    frames for subsequent ASCII conversion and grid layout.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:


    
  
  • uint32_t width (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • uint32_t height (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • rgb_pixel_t pixels[width * height] (RGB888 format)
    • 



PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Called at 30fps per active client
    • 
  
  • Uses zero-copy storage when possible
    • 
  
  • Validates packet size before processing
    • 
  
  • Implements frame counting for debug logging
    • 



STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Auto-enables client->is_sending_video if not already set
    • 
  
  • Increments client->frames_received counter
    • 
  
  • Updates client dimensions if changed
    • 



BUFFER MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Stores entire packet (including dimensions) in
      client->incoming_video_buffer
    • 
  
  • Uses multi-frame ringbuffer for burst handling
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow drops oldest frames (maintains real-time performance)
    • 
  
  • render.c threads consume frames for ASCII conversion
    • 



VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Packet size matches width * height * 3 + 8 bytes
    • 
  
  • Width and height are reasonable (prevents memory exhaustion)
    • 
  
  • Buffer pointers are valid before access
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packets are logged and dropped
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow is handled gracefully
    • 
  
  • Shutdown conditions don't generate error spam
    • 



PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS:


    
  
  • Debug logging is throttled (every 25000 frames)
    • 
  
  • Fast path for common case (valid packet with buffer space)
    • 
  
  • Minimal CPU work in receive thread (storage only)
    • 



Parameters


client Source client providing video data


data Packet payload containing image dimensions and RGB data


len Total size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This function is called by client receive threads at high
  frequency



Actual ASCII conversion happens in render threads (render.c)




Frame timestamps are added for synchronization purposes




See also


framebuffer_write_multi_frame() For buffer storage
  implementation



create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client() For frame
    consumption




Definition at line 735 of file
  server/protocol.c.


735                                                                               {
736   // Handle incoming image data from client
737   // New format: [width:4][height:4][compressed_flag:4][data_size:4][rgb_data:data_size]
738   // Old format: [width:4][height:4][rgb_data:w*h*3] (for backward compatibility)
739   // Use atomic compare-and-swap to avoid race condition - ensures thread-safe auto-enabling of video stream
740 
741   log_info("RECV_IMAGE_FRAME: client_id=%u, len=%zu", client->client_id, len);
742 
743   // Validate codec capability for RGBA frames
744   if (!VIDEO_CODEC_SUPPORTED(client->codec_capabilities_video, VIDEO_CODEC_RGBA)) {
745     log_error("Client %s sent RGBA frame but does not support VIDEO_CODEC_RGBA (capabilities=0x%x)",
746               client->client_id, client->codec_capabilities_video);
747     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "VIDEO_CODEC_RGBA not supported by client (capabilities=0x%x)",
748                                    client->codec_capabilities_video);
749     return;
750   }
751 
752   if (!data || len < sizeof(uint32_t) * 2) {
753     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME payload too small: %zu bytes", len);
754     return;
755   }
756   bool was_sending_video = atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_video);
757   if (!was_sending_video) {
758     // Try to atomically enable video sending
759     // Use atomic_compare_exchange_strong to avoid spurious failures
760     if (atomic_cas_bool(&client->is_sending_video, &was_sending_video, true)) {
761       log_info("Client %u auto-enabled video stream (received IMAGE_FRAME)", client->client_id);
762       // Notify client that their first video frame was received
763       if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
764         log_info_client(client, "First video frame received - streaming active");
765       }
766     }
767   } else {
768     // Log periodically to confirm we're receiving frames
769     // Use per-client counter protected by client_state_mutex to avoid race conditions
770     mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
771     client->frames_received_logged++;
772     if (client->frames_received_logged % 25000 == 0) {
773       char pretty[64];
774       format_bytes_pretty(len, pretty, sizeof(pretty));
775       log_debug("Client %u has sent %u IMAGE_FRAME packets (%s)", client->client_id, client->frames_received_logged,
776                 pretty);
777     }
778     mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
779   }
780 
781   // Parse image dimensions (use memcpy to avoid unaligned access)
782   uint32_t img_width_net, img_height_net;
783   memcpy(&img_width_net, data, sizeof(uint32_t));
784   memcpy(&img_height_net, (char *)data + sizeof(uint32_t), sizeof(uint32_t));
785   uint32_t img_width = NET_TO_HOST_U32(img_width_net);
786   uint32_t img_height = NET_TO_HOST_U32(img_height_net);
787 
788   log_debug("IMAGE_FRAME packet: width=%u, height=%u, payload_len=%zu", img_width, img_height, len);
789 
790   // Validate dimensions using image utility functions
791   if (image_validate_dimensions((size_t)img_width, (size_t)img_height) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
792     log_error("IMAGE_FRAME validation failed for dimensions: %u x %u", img_width, img_height);
793     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME invalid dimensions");
794     return;
795   }
796 
797   // Calculate RGB buffer size with overflow checking
798   size_t rgb_size = 0;
799   if (image_calc_rgb_size((size_t)img_width, (size_t)img_height, &rgb_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
800     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME buffer size calculation failed");
801     return;
802   }
803 
804   // Validate final buffer size against maximum
805   if (image_validate_buffer_size(rgb_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
806     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME buffer size exceeds maximum");
807     return;
808   }
809 
810   // Only support legacy format: [width:4][height:4][rgb_data:w*h*3]
811   if (rgb_size > SIZE_MAX - FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY) {
812     char size_str[32];
813     format_bytes_pretty(rgb_size, size_str, sizeof(size_str));
814     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME legacy packet size overflow: %s", size_str);
815     return;
816   }
817   size_t expected_size = FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY + rgb_size;
818 
819   if (len != expected_size) {
820     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME size mismatch: expected %zu bytes got %zu", expected_size, len);
821     return;
822   }
823 
824   // Validate legacy format
825   asciichat_error_t validate_result = frame_validate_legacy(len, rgb_size);
826   if (validate_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
827     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME legacy validation failed");
828     return;
829   }
830 
831   void *rgb_data = (char *)data + FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY;
832   size_t rgb_data_size = rgb_size;
833   bool needs_free = false;
834 
835   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
836     // Get the write buffer
837     video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_begin_write(client->incoming_video_buffer);
838 
839     if (frame && frame->data) {
840       // Build the packet in the old format for internal storage: [width:4][height:4][rgb_data:w*h*3]
841       // Use frame_check_size_overflow to validate overflow before repacking
842       asciichat_error_t overflow_check = frame_check_size_overflow(FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY, rgb_data_size);
843       if (overflow_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
844         if (needs_free && rgb_data) {
845           SAFE_FREE(rgb_data);
846         }
847         disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME size overflow while repacking");
848         return;
849       }
850       size_t old_packet_size = FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY + rgb_data_size;
851 
852       if (old_packet_size <= MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE) { // Max frame buffer size
853         uint32_t width_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32(img_width);
854         uint32_t height_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32(img_height);
855 
856         // Pack in old format for internal consistency
857         memcpy(frame->data, &width_net, sizeof(uint32_t));
858         memcpy((char *)frame->data + sizeof(uint32_t), &height_net, sizeof(uint32_t));
859         memcpy((char *)frame->data + sizeof(uint32_t) * 2, rgb_data, rgb_data_size);
860 
861         frame->size = old_packet_size;
862         frame->width = img_width;
863         frame->height = img_height;
864         frame->capture_timestamp_ns = (uint64_t)time(NULL) * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
865         frame->sequence_number = ++client->frames_received;
866 
867         // DEBUG: Compute hash of incoming RGB data to detect duplicates
868         uint32_t incoming_rgb_hash = 0;
869         for (size_t i = 0; i < rgb_data_size && i < 1000; i++) {
870           incoming_rgb_hash = (uint32_t)((uint64_t)incoming_rgb_hash * 31 + ((unsigned char *)rgb_data)[i]);
871         }
872 
873         // Per-client hash tracking (not static!) to avoid cross-client interference
874         const char *client_id = client->client_id;
875         bool is_new_frame = (incoming_rgb_hash != client->last_received_frame_hash);
876 
877         if (is_new_frame) {
878           log_info("RECV_FRAME #%u NEW: Client %u size=%zu dims=%ux%u hash=0x%08x (prev=0x%08x)",
879                    client->frames_received, client_id, rgb_data_size, img_width, img_height, incoming_rgb_hash,
880                    client->last_received_frame_hash);
881           client->last_received_frame_hash = incoming_rgb_hash;
882         } else {
883           log_info("RECV_FRAME #%u DUP: Client %u size=%zu dims=%ux%u hash=0x%08x", client->frames_received, client_id,
884                    rgb_data_size, img_width, img_height, incoming_rgb_hash);
885         }
886 
887         video_frame_commit(client->incoming_video_buffer);
888       } else {
889         if (needs_free && rgb_data) {
890           SAFE_FREE(rgb_data);
891         }
892         disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME repacked frame too large (%zu bytes)", old_packet_size);
893         return;
894       }
895     } else {
896       log_warn("Failed to get write buffer for client %u (frame=%p, frame->data=%p)", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
897                frame ? frame->data : NULL);
898     }
899   } else {
900     // During shutdown, this is expected - don't spam error logs
901     if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
902       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Client %u has no incoming video buffer!", client->client_id);
903     } else {
904       log_debug("Client %u: ignoring video packet during shutdown", client->client_id);
905     }
906   }
907 
908   // Clean up decompressed data if allocated
909   if (needs_free && rgb_data) {
910     SAFE_FREE(rgb_data);
911   }
912 }






References atomic_cas_bool(), atomic_load_bool(),
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(), format_bytes_pretty(),
    frame, frame_check_size_overflow(),
    frame_validate_legacy(), g_should_exit,
    image_calc_rgb_size(), image_validate_buffer_size(),
    image_validate_dimensions(), video_frame_begin_write(), and
    video_frame_commit().






void
  handle_ping_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Handle PING packet - respond with PONG.


Definition at line 645 of file
  server/protocol.c.


645                                                                              {
646   (void)data;
647   (void)len;
648 
649   // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
650   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
651   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
652     mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
653     return;
654   }
655   acip_transport_t *pong_transport = client->transport;
656   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
657 
658   // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
659   asciichat_error_t pong_result = acip_send_pong(pong_transport);
660   if (pong_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
661     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send PONG response to client %u: %s", client->client_id,
662               asciichat_error_string(pong_result));
663   }
664 }

  atomic_load_bool().

  handle_pong_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)

  server/protocol.c.


669                                                                              {

671   (void)data;

void
  handle_protocol_version_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process PROTOCOL_VERSION packet - validate protocol compatibility.
    Clients send this packet to announce their protocol version and
    capabilities. The server validates that the major version matches and logs
    any version mismatches for debugging purposes.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
protocol_version_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
protocol_version: Major version number (must match
    PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR)

  
 •

  
protocol_revision: Minor version number

  
 •

  
supports_encryption: Encryption capability flag

  
 •

  
compression_algorithms: Supported compression bitmask

  
 •

  
feature_flags: Optional feature flags




VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Packet size matches sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)
    • 
  
  • Major protocol version matches (PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR)
    • 
  
  • Reserved bytes are zero (future-proofing)
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Version mismatch is logged but NOT fatal (backward compatibility)
    • 
  
  • Invalid packet size triggers disconnect
    • 



Parameters


client Client that sent the packet


data Packet payload (protocol_version_packet_t)


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This is typically the first packet in the handshake



Protocol version validation ensures compatibility




Definition at line 359 of file
  server/protocol.c.


359                                                                                          {
360   if (!data) {
361     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "PROTOCOL_VERSION payload missing");
362     return;
363   }
364 
365   if (len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
366     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "PROTOCOL_VERSION invalid size: %zu (expected %zu)", len,
367                                    sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
368     return;
369   }
370 
371   const protocol_version_packet_t *version = (const protocol_version_packet_t *)data;
372   uint16_t client_major = NET_TO_HOST_U16(version->protocol_version);
373   uint16_t client_minor = NET_TO_HOST_U16(version->protocol_revision);
374 
375   // Validate major version match (minor version can differ for backward compat)
376   if (client_major != PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR) {
377     log_warn("Client %u protocol version mismatch: client=%u.%u, server=%u.%u", client->client_id, client_major,
378              client_minor, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MINOR);
379     // Note: We don't disconnect on version mismatch for backward compatibility
380     // Clients may be older or newer than server
381   } else if (client_minor != PROTOCOL_VERSION_MINOR) {
382     log_info("Client %u has different protocol revision: client=%u.%u, server=%u.%u", client->client_id, client_major,
383              client_minor, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MINOR);
384   }
385 
386   // Validate reserved bytes are zero
387   for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(version->reserved); i++) {
388     if (version->reserved[i] != 0) {
389       log_warn("Client %u sent non-zero reserved bytes in PROTOCOL_VERSION packet", client->client_id);
390       // Don't disconnect - reserved bytes may be used in future versions
391       break;
392     }
393   }
394 
395   // Log supported features
396   if (ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(version->supports_encryption)) {
397     log_debug("Client %u supports encryption", client->client_id);
398   }
399   if (version->compression_algorithms != 0) {
400     log_debug("Client %u supports compression: 0x%02x", client->client_id, version->compression_algorithms);
401   }
402   if (version->feature_flags != 0) {
403     uint16_t feature_flags = NET_TO_HOST_U16(version->feature_flags);
404     log_debug("Client %u supports features: 0x%04x", client->client_id, feature_flags);
405   }
406 }






References disconnect_client_for_bad_data().






void
  handle_remote_log_packet_from_client (client_info_t * client, const void *
  data, size_t len)


Definition at line 1107 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1107                                                                                                {
1108   if (!client) {
1109     return;
1110   }
1111 
1112   log_level_t remote_level = LOG_INFO;
1113   remote_log_direction_t direction = REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN;
1114   uint16_t flags = 0;
1115   char message[MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH + 1] = {0};
1116 
1117   asciichat_error_t parse_result =
1118       packet_parse_remote_log(data, len, &remote_level, &direction, &flags, message, sizeof(message), NULL);
1119   if (parse_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1120     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Invalid REMOTE_LOG packet: %s", asciichat_error_string(parse_result));
1121     return;
1122   }
1123 
1124   if (direction != REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER) {
1125     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "REMOTE_LOG direction mismatch: %u", direction);
1126     return;
1127   }
1128 
1129   const bool truncated = (flags & REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_TRUNCATED) != 0;
1130   const char *display_name = client->display_name[0] ? client->display_name : "(unnamed)";
1131   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
1132 
1133   if (truncated) {
1134     log_msg(remote_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, "[REMOTE CLIENT %u \"%s\"] %s [message truncated]", client_id,
1135             display_name, message);
1136   } else {
1137     log_msg(remote_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, "[REMOTE CLIENT %u \"%s\"] %s", client_id, display_name,
1138             message);
1139   }
1140 }






References disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    log_msg(), and packet_parse_remote_log().






void
  handle_size_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process terminal size update packet - handle client window resize.
    Clients send this packet when their terminal window is resized, allowing the
    server to adjust ASCII frame dimensions accordingly. This ensures optimal
    use of the client's display area.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
size_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
uint16_t width: New terminal width in characters

  
 •

  
uint16_t height: New terminal height in characters




STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Updates client->width with new dimensions
    • 
  
  • Updates client->height with new dimensions
    • 
  
  • Thread-safe update using client state mutex
    • 



RENDERING IMPACT:


    
  
  • Subsequent ASCII frames will use new dimensions
    • 
  
  • Grid layout calculations will incorporate new size
    • 
  
  • No immediate frame regeneration (happens on next cycle)
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packet sizes are ignored silently
    • 
  
  • Extreme dimensions are accepted (client responsibility)
    • 
  
  • Concurrent updates are handled safely
    • 



Parameters


client Target client whose terminal was resized


data Packet payload containing new dimensions


len Size of packet payload (should be sizeof(size_packet_t))


Note


Changes take effect on the next rendering cycle



No validation of reasonable dimension ranges




Definition at line 1788 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1788                                                                              {
1789   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(size_packet_t), "SIZE");
1790 
1791   const size_packet_t *size_pkt = (const size_packet_t *)data;
1792 
1793   // Extract and validate new dimensions
1794   uint16_t width = NET_TO_HOST_U16(size_pkt->width);
1795   uint16_t height = NET_TO_HOST_U16(size_pkt->height);
1796 
1797   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, width, "width", "SIZE");
1798   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, height, "height", "SIZE");
1799   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, width, 1, 4096, "width", "SIZE");
1800   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, height, 1, 4096, "height", "SIZE");
1801 
1802   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1803   client->width = width;
1804   client->height = height;
1805   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1806 
1807   log_info("Client %u updated terminal size: %ux%u", client->client_id, width, height);
1808 }










void
  handle_stream_start_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process STREAM_START packet - client requests to begin media
    transmission. Clients send this packet to indicate they're ready to start
    sending video and/or audio data. The server updates its internal state to
    expect and process media packets from this client.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:


    
  
  • uint32_t stream_type (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • Bitmask containing STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO and/or STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO
    • 



STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • VIDEO: Records intention to send video (is_sending_video set by first
      IMAGE_FRAME)
    • 
  
  • AUDIO: Sets client->is_sending_audio = true if STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO
      present
    • 
  
  • Enables render threads to include this client in output generation
    • 



PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Client must have announced capabilities via CLIENT_JOIN first
    • 
  
  • Server will start processing IMAGE_FRAME and AUDIO packets
    • 
  
  • Render threads will begin generating output for this client
    • 
  
  • Grid layout will be recalculated to include this client
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Ignores packets with incorrect size
    • 
  
  • Invalid stream types are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • Graceful handling if client lacks necessary capabilities
    • 



Parameters


client Target client starting media transmission


data Packet payload containing stream type flags


len Size of packet payload (should be sizeof(uint32_t))


Note


Changes take effect immediately for subsequent media
  packets



Render threads will detect the state change on their next
  cycle




See also


handle_stream_stop_packet() For stopping media
  transmission



handle_image_frame_packet() For video data processing




Definition at line 521 of file
  server/protocol.c.


521                                                                                      {
522   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(uint32_t), "STREAM_START");
523 
524   uint32_t stream_type_net;
525   memcpy(&stream_type_net, data, sizeof(uint32_t));
526   uint32_t stream_type = NET_TO_HOST_U32(stream_type_net);
527 
528   // Validate at least one stream type flag is set
529   const uint32_t VALID_STREAM_MASK = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO | STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO;
530   VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_START");
531 
532   // Validate no unknown stream type bits are set
533   VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_START");
534 
535   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
536     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, true);
537   }
538   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
539     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, true);
540 
541     // Create Opus decoder for this client if not already created
542     if (!client->opus_decoder) {
543       client->opus_decoder = opus_codec_create_decoder(48000);
544       if (client->opus_decoder) {
545         log_info("Client %u: Opus decoder created (48kHz)", client->client_id);
546       } else {
547         log_error("Client %u: Failed to create Opus decoder", client->client_id);
548       }
549     }
550   }
551 
552   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
553     log_info("Client %u announced video stream (waiting for first frame)", client->client_id);
554   }
555   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
556     log_info("Client %u started audio stream", client->client_id);
557   }
558 
559   // Notify client of stream start acknowledgment
560   const char *streams = (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) && (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO)
561                             ? "video+audio"
562                             : ((stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) ? "video" : "audio");
563   // Only send remote log to TCP clients (WebSocket clients have invalid socket)
564   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
565     log_info_client(client, "Stream started: %s", streams);
566   }
567 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
    opus_codec_create_decoder().






void
  handle_stream_stop_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process STREAM_STOP packet - client requests to halt media
    transmission. Clients send this packet to gracefully stop sending video
    and/or audio data. The server updates its state to exclude this client from
    active media processing and grid layout calculations.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:


    
  
  • uint32_t stream_type (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • Bitmask containing STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO and/or STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO
    • 



STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Sets client->is_sending_video = false if STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO present
    • 
  
  • Sets client->is_sending_audio = false if STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO present
    • 
  
  • Render threads will stop including this client in output
    • 



PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Client remains connected but won't appear in video grid
    • 
  
  • Existing buffered media from this client will still be processed
    • 
  
  • Grid layout recalculates to exclude this client
    • 
  
  • Client can restart streaming with STREAM_START packet
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Ignores packets with incorrect size
    • 
  
  • Invalid stream types are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • Safe to call multiple times or when not streaming
    • 



Parameters


client Target client stopping media transmission


data Packet payload containing stream type flags


len Size of packet payload (should be sizeof(uint32_t))


Note


Changes take effect immediately



Render threads will detect the state change on their next
  cycle




See also


handle_stream_start_packet() For starting media
  transmission


Definition at line 604 of file
  server/protocol.c.


604                                                                                     {
605   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(uint32_t), "STREAM_STOP");
606 
607   uint32_t stream_type_net;
608   memcpy(&stream_type_net, data, sizeof(uint32_t));
609   uint32_t stream_type = NET_TO_HOST_U32(stream_type_net);
610 
611   // Validate at least one stream type flag is set
612   const uint32_t VALID_STREAM_MASK = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO | STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO;
613   VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_STOP");
614 
615   // Validate no unknown stream type bits are set
616   VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_STOP");
617 
618   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
619     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
620   }
621   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
622     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
623   }
624 
625   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
626     log_info("Client %u stopped video stream", client->client_id);
627   }
628   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
629     log_info("Client %u stopped audio stream", client->client_id);
630   }
631 
632   // Notify client of stream stop acknowledgment
633   const char *streams = (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) && (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO)
634                             ? "video+audio"
635                             : ((stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) ? "video" : "audio");
636   // Only send remote log to TCP clients (WebSocket clients have invalid socket)
637   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
638     log_info_client(client, "Stream stopped: %s", streams);
639   }
640 }






References atomic_store_bool().






int
  send_server_state_to_client (client_info_t * client)


Send current server state to a specific client. Generates and
    queues a SERVER_STATE packet containing information about the current number
    of connected and active clients. This helps clients understand the
    multi-user environment and adjust their behavior accordingly.




PACKET CONTENT GENERATED:



  
•

  
server_state_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
connected_client_count: Total clients connected to server

  
 •

  
active_client_count: Clients actively sending video/audio

  
 •

  
reserved: Padding for future extensions




USAGE SCENARIOS:


    
  
  • Initial state after client joins server
    • 
  
  • Periodic updates when client count changes
    • 
  
  • Response to client requests for server information
    • 



IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:


    
  
  • Counts active clients by scanning global client manager
    • 
  
  • Converts data to network byte order before queuing
    • 
  
  • Uses client's video queue for delivery
    • 
  
  • Non-blocking operation (queues for later delivery)
    • 



THREAD SAFETY:


    
  
  • Uses reader lock on global client manager
    • 
  
  • Safe to call from any thread
    • 
  
  • Atomic snapshot of client counts
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Returns -1 if client or queue is invalid
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow handled by packet_queue_enqueue()
    • 
  
  • No side effects on failure
    • 



Parameters


client Target client to receive server state
  information


Returns


0 on successful queuing, -1 on error


Note


Packet delivery happens asynchronously via send thread



Client count may change between queuing and delivery




See also


broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients() For
  multi-client updates


Definition at line 1862 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1862                                                        {
1863   if (!client) {
1864     return -1;
1865   }
1866 
1867   // Count active clients - LOCK OPTIMIZATION: Use atomic reads, no rwlock needed
1868   int active_count = 0;
1869   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1870     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1871       active_count++;
1872     }
1873   }
1874 
1875   // Prepare server state packet
1876   server_state_packet_t state;
1877   state.connected_client_count = active_count;
1878   state.active_client_count = active_count; // For now, all connected are active
1879   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1880 
1881   // Convert to network byte order
1882   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1883   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1884   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1885   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1886 
1887   // Send server state via ACIP transport
1888   // Protect socket writes with send_mutex to prevent race with send_thread.
1889   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1890   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1891   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1892 
1893   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1894     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send server state to client %u: %s", client->client_id,
1895               asciichat_error_string(result));
1896     return -1;
1897   }
1898 
1899   log_debug("Sent server state to client %u: %u connected, %u active", client->client_id, state.connected_client_count,
1900             state.active_client_count);
1901   return 0;
1902 }






References acip_send_server_state(),
    atomic_load_bool(), and g_client_manager.
